title: (No AI) South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘ RAMageddon’

title: (No AI) Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
author: Hayden Field
content:
![Illustration of a password above a closed combination lock.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25299197/STK442_Password_Manager_A_CVirginia.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A new proposal would ban the sale of Americans' health and location information to data brokers - including information people reveal to an AI chatbot like ChatGPT or Claude.

In the coming weeks, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon (D-PA) are planning to debut a new version of the Health and Location Data Protection Act that's better suited to the AI era. The former version of the bill, first introduced in [June 2022](https://www.warren.senate.gov/news/press-releases/warren-wyden-murray-whitehouse-sanders-introduce-legislation-to-ban-data-brokers-from-selling-americans-location-and-health-data/), prohibited data brokers from collecting and selling health and location data. Four years later, it's expanded to ban other companies from selling such data to brokers, and to specifically cover …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/959033/health-location-data-protection-act-ai-warren-scanlon)

A new proposal would ban the sale of Americans' health and location information to data brokers - including information people reveal to an AI chatbot like ChatGPT or Claude. 
In the coming weeks, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon (D-PA) are planning to debut a new version of the Health and Location Data Protection Act that's better suited to the AI era. The former version of the bill, first introduced in June 2022, prohibited data brokers from collecting and selling health and location data. Four years later, it's expanded to ban other companies from selling such data to brokers, and to specifically cover  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![A grenade overlayed with ACSII art.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STKS515_CYBER_WARFARE2_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

China's Zhipu AI ([Z.ai](http://z.ai)) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it [matches Mythos](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/chinese-ai-anthropic-mythos-cybersecurity-574b02c2?st=FRKgap&reflink=desktopwebshare_permalink) in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from [Anthropic](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/957845/openai-gpt-5-6-trump-administration-ai-preview) and OpenAI in other, more general tasks, it seems that China has dramatically reduced the gap in the capabilities between its models and those of the US.

This level of advancement is particularly concerning to the US government, which has worked to restrict China's access to powerful models like Anthropic's [Mythos](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/958458/anthropic-mythos-5-is-back-trump-negotiations) and Fable, as well as the hardware necessary to train and run them. The Trump administration views Mythos and other advan …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/958804/chinas-z-ai-glm-52-mythos-cybersecurity)

China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from Anthropic and OpenAI in other, more general tasks, it seems that China has dramatically reduced the gap in the capabilities between its models and those of the US.
This level of advancement is particularly concerning to the US government, which has worked to restrict China's access to powerful models like Anthropic's Mythos and Fable, as well as the hardware necessary to train and run them. The Trump administration views Mythos and other advan …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Maxwell Zeff
title: (No AI) Trump Administration Allows Anthropic to Release  Mythos to Select US Organizations
dc:creator: Maxwell Zeff
content: After weeks of negotiations, the White House permitted Anthropic to grant access to its most advanced AI model to a select group of US companies and government agencies.
contentSnippet: After weeks of negotiations, the White House permitted Anthropic to grant access to its most advanced AI model to a select group of US companies and government agencies.
